summary: Well-scoped plan that adds a hint registry as a typed single source of truth and renders it in the existing overlay. No new dependencies, no architectural boundary crossings, no privacy concerns. The compile-time completeness check via mapped-type assertion is a sound approach for keeping hints aligned with VisualParams. The two existing test updates (line ~222 regex and line ~229 char budget) are correctly identified and their mitigations are appropriate.

risks_assessment.risk: Removing p3 breaks US-022 overlay text test at line 222
risks_assessment.note: Plan correctly identifies the exact regex and proposes checking hint list content instead

risks_assessment.risk: 500-char budget test at line 229 counts all childNodes excluding close button
risks_assessment.note:
Scoping to

elements or raising the budget for structured list content is reasonable

risks_assessment.risk: Future VisualParams fields not covered by hints
risks_assessment.note: TypeScript mapped-type assertion will produce a compile error if a new key is added without a corresponding hint

notes: The plan is minimal and well-aligned with the codebase. VisualParams has exactly 8 keys matching the 6 hint entries' paramKeys union. The overlay construction pattern (createElement + appendChild) is consistent with the existing infoOverlay.ts style. No concerns about privacy, performance, or maintenance burden.
